ksa_text: Skill in conducting iterative AI risk assessments across the system lifecycle, including risk identification and analysis at development and deployment phases, impact and likelihood estimation with proportionate documentation, and maintenance of risk registries with proxy indicators for unmeasurable risks.

dcwf.top_element_label: Skill in conducting audits or reviews of technical systems.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Workforce diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility processes are prioritized in the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks throughout the lifecycle.

top_anchors.element_label: Workforce diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility processes are prioritized in the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks throughout the lifecycle.
Skill in conducting audits or reviews of technical systems.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA AG-S-002 (AI Governance/Skill) scores 0.585 composite (highest: NIST AI RMF at 0.585) and is edge_case for the AI Research Scientist role. Core role signal: 0 pattern match(es). Seniority is appropriate for this role.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency identified. Include consistent with edge_case scope, NIST AI RMF signal (0.585), and AI Governance handling. Decision stands.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: AI Governance for AI Research Scientist IC-principal?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: AI research scientists increasingly engage with alignment/safety topics (AI Governance adjacent). Technical AI safety, alignment research, and model cards may be in scope at principal level. Governance ownership and policy authorship remain out of scope.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Include upheld. AI research scientists increasingly engage with alignment/safety topics (AI Governance adjacent). Final decision: include.
